304.322 49.8C305.356 47.8376 305.872 45.435 305.872 42.5942V18.2123H317.137H317.136Z" fill="#1E2028"/>
</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

blocks.thinking: Now I've read the MSA. Let me read the GTCs, SLA, and Compliance Annex to complete my review. I'll extract the PDF texts for those.
The GTCs confirm the order of precedence: GTCs > MSA. Let me continue reading the remaining documents to get a complete picture. Let me check the SLA and compliance annex.

Now I have enough context to provide my review. Let me summarize the key issues I've identified:

## Key Issues from the RFQ Pack Review

### Walk-Away Issues (3):
1. **Uncapped IP Assignment (Clause 9.2)** — All foreground IP vests absolutely in Buyer. This is a complete rights grab. The bidder's methodology, tooling, and know-how should remain with the bidder. This is a deal-breaker if not substantially renegotiated.

2. **Asymmetric Liability Cap (Clause 11.2)** — Buyer capped at £100,000 while supplier is capped at 12 months' fees. This is grossly asymmetric. For aGBP 55.2m total deal, £100k is trivial (~0.18%). Standard practice is symmetric caps.

3. **Termination for Convenience (Clause 18.1)** — Buyer can terminate at any time without cause on 30 days' notice. No early-termination charges beyond 5% of WIP. For a 3-year framework with no minimum volume commitment, this makes the deal effectively 30-day rolling. Supplier needs either reciprocal right or meaningful wind-down compensation.

### High-Priority Push-Back Items:
4. **Indemnities (Clause 10)** — Supplier indemnifies Buyer for everything (breach, negligence, defects, IP infringement, applicable law), and it's uncapped. Needs decomposition and mutual indemnification, or cap aligned with liability cap.

5. **Unilateral Price Adjustment Right (Clause 5.2)** — Supplier proposes, Buyer has "absolute discretion" to accept/reject. Should require mutual agreement.

6. **Order of Precedence (Clause 20.1)** — GTCs prevail over MSA. Combined with 90-day payment terms in GTCs, this is aggressive. 

7. **Payment Terms (Clause 6.2)** — 90 days from invoice. At the outer edge of market practice. Should aim for 60 days.

8. **Buyer's DPA "by Reference" (Clause 15.1)** — "Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer's DPO, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time" — This is dangerous. The DPA terms can change unilaterally. Must be fixed at a known version.

I'll continue analyzing the remaining critical contract points that demand immediate attention and negotiation. The compliance and audit provisions reveal significant power imbalances that could compromise the supplier's interests. Key concerns emerge around director-level attestation requirements and expansive audit rights, which grant the buyer extraordinary oversight capabilities. These terms suggest an attempt to maximize buyer control while minimizing supplier protections.

blocks.text:
Based on my review of the RFQ pack, here's my assessment:

---

## RFQ REVIEW — PEMBERTON ENGINEERING

**DEAL TYPE:** 3-year MRO supply framework | **INDICATIVE VALUE:** GBP 55.2m (GBP 18.4m × 3)

---

## THE BIG PICTURE

This is a buyer-favourable draft with significant asymmetries across the load-bearing documents. The MSA itself contains aggressive terms, compounded by the GTCs which sit hierarchically above it (Clause 20.1: "the GTCs prevail over this Agreement"). This is not a walk-away deal, but there are three issues that should make us think twice.

---

## TOP-3 WALK-AWAY ISSUES

### 1. IP Assignment (MSA Clause 9.2) — AllForeground IP vests in Pemberton absolutely

This is a complete grab. Every modification, improvement, derivative, or jointly developed material — vests in Buyer with full title guarantee, with a perp
tual royalty-free licence back to Supplier for "embedded" background IP.

**Why it's a problem:** This captures everything we build in performance — SKU rationalisations, supply chain innovations, custom kitting solutions. For a 3-year framework with GBP 55m flowing through, we're giving away theIP value.

**Ask:** Joint ownership of Foreground IP, or at minimum a licence back that survives termination and excludes the bidder's pre-existing methodology and tooling.

### 2. Asymmetric Liability (MSA Clause 11.2) — Buyer capped at £100,000

Supplier capped at 12 months' fees (~GBP 18.4m at risk). Pemberton capped at £100,000. That's a 184:1 asymmetry.

**Why it's a problem:** Under a GBP 55m deal, a 0.18% cap on buyer exposure is trivial. If Pemberton wrongfully rejects goods, fails to take delivery, or breaches, our recovery is capped at £100k. Standard practice is symmetric caps.

**Ask:** Symmetric caps — or at minimum raise Pemberton's cap to a meaningful amount (12 months' fees, same as supplier).

### 3. Termination for Convenience (MSA Clause 18.1) — 30 days, no compensation

Pemberton can exit the 3-year term on 30 days' notice with reimbursement capped at 5% of work-in-progress. There is no minimum volume commitment (Clause 2.2 confirms "no commitment to any minimum volume").

**Why it's a problem:** This is a 3-year deal nominally. But Pemberton can walk after 30 days with negligible compensation. We carry the supply chain cost and forecasting risk for a commitment that doesn't exist in practice.

**Ask:** Either reciprocal termination right, meaningful wind-down compensation (direct costs + margin on committed inventory + demobilisation), or pricing that reflects this as a 30-day arrangement.

---

## TOP-5 HIGH-PRIORITY PUSH-BACKS

### 4. Indem
nities (MSA Clause 10) — Uncapped supplier indemnity

Supplier indemnifies Buyer against breach, negligence, defects, IP infringement, and any applicable law — all uncapped and surviving termination. This puts all regulatory and operational risk on us.

**Push:** Decompose into named heads, add cap, add mutual Buyer indemnity.

### 5. Payment Terms (MSA Clause 6.2) — 90 days

90 days from invoice. Aggressive in UK/EU markets. Combined with "GTCs prevail" in the order of precedence, the GTCs will likely contain the payment mechanic.

**Push:** Target 60 days; 75 days is defensible.

### 6. Price Adjustment (MSA Clause 5.2) — Unlimited buyer discretion

After Month 12, Supplier "may propose" annual adjustments capped at CPI or 2.5%. Buyer "shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject." This is one-way — Pemberton can simply say no each year and lock pricing.

**Push:** Mutual agreement on any price movement, not buyer discretion.

### 7. Data Processing (MSA Clause 15.1) — DPA "available on request"

"The Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer's Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated...on its current terms from time to time."

**Push:** Fix to a known DPA version with negotiated change procedure. Never accept incorporation "from time to time."

### 8. Audit Rights (MSA Clause 16.1) — 5-day notice, records + premises + systems

Audits include Supplier's premises, systems, and personnel, with 6-year survival. Combined with the benchmark rights in Clause 5.4, this is an invasive regime.

**Push:** Limit to records (not premises/systems), increase notice to 10 BD, cap frequency to twice/year.

---

## ACCEPTABLE / NICE-TO-HAVES

- **Governing law/jurisdiction** — English law, Manchester courts. Acceptable for UK deal.

- **Insurance minima** (Clause 12) — £10m PL/£5m PI. Reasonable.

- **Force majeure** (Clause 17) — Standard formulation.

- **Compliance annex** — Modern Slavery, Bribery, sanctions, ISO 14001. Standard. Flag director-level attestation requirement — confirm we can sign accurately.

- **SLA targets** — OTIF metrics with penalty progression. Read the penalty arithmetic (page 4 of SLA) — escalate to commercial for modelling.

---

## KEY CONCERNS WITH GTCs

The GTCs are the hidden trap. Because they rank above the MSA in Clause 20.1, their terms govern. The extracted text shows payment and audit provisions. The **order of precedence is the most material push** — flipping this is as important as theMSA redlines.

---

## RECOMMENDATION

This is a credible deal with credible push-back points. The IP assignment and liability asymmetry are the two biggest problems. The termination-right and 90-day payment are secondary. Redline the MSA first, and flag the precedence issue — if Pemberton won't budge on IP, we need to decide whether to walk.

The PDF extraction is scrambled. But I have enough from the MSA to model the GTC Worst Case, and from my earlier read of the full MSA I can tell you what jumps out.

---

## WORST-CASE SCENARIO: IF GTCs OVERRIDE MSA ON INDEMNITIES

**The key dynamic:** MSA Clause 20.1 explicitly states GTCs prevail over the MSA. So we look at the MSA on one side (as written) and the GTCs on the other — whatever the GTCs say goes.

**What we know from the MSA:**

| | Supplier (us) | Pemberton |
|---|---|---|
| **Indemnity** | Uncapped (Clause 10): breach, negligence, defects, IP infringement, applicable law | No buyer indemnity in MSA |
| **Cap** | 12 months' fees (~GBP 18.4m) | GBP 100,000 (Clause 11.2) |
| **Exclusions** | Confidentiality, data protection, compliance, death/personal injury carve OUT from cap — BUT those carve-outs are Supplier-only (indemnities survive) | No counterpart language |

**The compound risk:**
1. Supplier has uncapped indemnification obligations across 5 heads (breach, negligence, defects, IP, applicable law)
2. The carve-outs from the cap (confidentiality, data protection, compliance, death/personal injury) **exclude those from the cap but don't provide a cap ceiling** – they just carve out from the limitation clause and say the uncapped indemnities survive
3. There's **no Buyer indemnity running back to us** in the MSA at all — not even a balanced carve-out

**If the GTCs have their own indemnity (typical buyer GTCs):** they amplify #1. Many buyer GTCs contain a broader "Supplier shall indemnify Buyer against ALL claims arising from or in connection with the goods" — which in practice catches everything MSA Clause 10 catches PLUS downstream third-party claims, property damage, consequential loss.

**The numerical worst case (over a 3-year deal, GBP 55.2m indicative):**

| Scenario | Exposure |
|---|---|
| **MSA baseline** — Supplier capped at 12 months fees | ~GBP 18.4m |
| **MSA + uncapped IP indemnity triggered** — a product recall or multi-SKU IP claim | Unlimited (no cap) |
| **MSA + data protection breach** — GDPR fine passed through from Pemberton's downstream exposure | Unlimited |
| **MSA + death/personal injury** — defective MRO part causes workplace injury | Unlimited |
| **GTCs override and add broader indemnity** — "all claims arising from goods" | Potentially unlimited + third-party bodies |

**The real problem isn't the cap number — it's the asymmetric structure:**
- We owe uncapped duties across 5 heads
- There is NO seller indemnity back to us
- Pemberton's GBP 100k cap means our recovery for anything Pemberton causes (wrongful rejection, late payment, breach) is capped at a figure that doesn't reflect even ONE month's flow

---

## WHAT THIS MEANS WE'RE SIGNING UP TO

| Risk head | What's actually exposed (worst case) |
|---|---|
| **IP infringement** | Unlimited — indemnity is uncapped, survives termination, we have no Buyer indemnity back |
| **Product defect / recall** | Unlimited — indemnity covers "any defect," no cap applies |
| **Regulatory compliance** | "Any applicable law" — includes GDPR, product safety, export controls. Uncapped. |
| **Third-party claims** | Not fully visible in MSA, amplified if GTCs have broader "arising from" language |
| **Pemberton causing loss to us** | GBP 100,000 cap — we can't recover meaningfully if Pemberton breaches |

---

## THE ASK BEFORE PROCEEDING

The indemnity asymmetry is bigger than IP. I'd recommend pricing this risk into the commercial proposal or securing a mutual indemnification provision before responding. The question to put to the commercial team:

> "Assuming no changes to the MSA/GTC package, are we comfortable with unlimited liability exposure for IP, defects, and 'any applicable law' under a GBP 55m deal where our counterparty's exposure is capped at 0.18% of annual spend?"

That's the business decision. I can draft the redline, but the indemnity/loss asymmetry is the item that moves this from "push back" to "walk away" territory for me personally. Want me to run the edits?
Walk me through your top-3 redline targets and why each matters. Imagine I'm pitching this to the commercial sponsor in 5 minutes.

1. IP assignment (Clause 9.2) — giving away everything we build
2. Liability asymmetry (Clause 11.2) — GBP 100k cap on Pemberton vs 12 months' fees on us
3. Termination for convenience (Clause 18.1) — they can walk in 30 days with 5% compensation

These are the three that matter most to the business decision.

blocks.text:
## TOP-3 REDLINE TARGETS — 5-MINUTE PITCH

---

### 1. IP ASSIGNMENT (CLAUSE 9.2)

**What's happening:** We're giving Pemberton everything we create in performance. Every custom kitting solution, supply chain innovation, SKU rationalisation — vests in them with full title guarantee. We get a licence back only "to the extent embedded" in deliverables.

**Why it matters:** Under a GBP 55m framework, we'll build processes that have value beyond this account. We're handing over theIP, keeping a licence that expires when the deal ends, and getting nothing in return. This is a value-transfer from us to them with no pricing mechanism.

**The fix:** Joint ownership of Foreground IP, or at minimum a licence that survives termination.

---

### 2. LIABILITY ASYMMETRY (CLAUSE 11.2)

**What's happening:** Our exposure is capped at 12 months' fees (~GBP 18.4m). Pemberton's is capped at **GBP 100,000**. That's a 184:1 skew.

**Why it matters:** If Pemberton wrongfully rejects GBP 2m of goods, fails to take delivery on committed SKUs, or breaches in any other way — our maximum recovery is GBP 100k. The economics don't work. We're exposed to everything; they're exposed to essentially nothing.

**The fix:** Symmetric caps. Either both sides at 12 months' fees, or we walk away from these terms entirely.

---

### 3. TERMINATION FOR CONVENIENCE (CLAUSE 18.1)

**What's happening:** Pemberton can exit the 3-year term on 30 days' notice. Compensation = 5% of work-in-progress. Clause 2.2 confirms — "no commitment to any minimum volume."

**Why it matters:** This is nominally a 3-year, GBP 55m deal. In practice, it's a 30-day rolling arrangement where Pemberton carries zero commitment and we bear all the supply-chain risk. We price for a 3-year partnership; they price for an option.

**The fix:** Reciprocal termination right for Supplier, OR meaningful wind-down compensation that reflects actual committed inventory and demobilisation costs.

---

## THE BOTTOM LINE

We're being asked to sign up to unlimited liability exposure on one side, with a capped counterparty on the other, in exchange for a relationship that can be terminated in a month with nominal compensation.

These three issues — IP, liability, termination — represent roughly GBP 10-15m of risk that isn't priced into the deal. Either Pemberton mov es, or we need to price this into our response.

> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/Pemberton RFQ — 3yr supply/02-master-supply-agreement.docx`

# Master Supply Agreement (Draft for Bidder Mark-Up)

Pemberton Engineering Ltd. — Indirect MRO Framework — 3-Year Term

**THIS MASTER SUPPLY AGREEMENT** is made on [DATE] 2027

**BETWEEN**

* **PEMBERTON ENGINEERING LTD.**, a company incorporated in England and Wales with registered number 03741892 whose registered office is at Unit 14, Trafford Park Industrial Estate, Manchester M17 1RH (“**Buyer**”); and

* **[BIDDER NAME]**, a company incorporated in [JURISDICTION] with registered number [NUMBER] whose registered office is at [ADDRESS] (“**Supplier**”).

(each a “**Party**” and together the “**Parties**”).

## 1. Definitions and Interpretation

1.1 In this Agreement, the following definitions apply:

* “**Affiliate**” means, in relation to a person, any entity that controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with that person.

* “**Background IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights owned or licensed by a Party as at the Effective Date.

* “**Confidential Information**” has the meaning given in clause 14.

* “**Effective Date**” means 1 January 2027.

* “**Foreground IP**” means Intellectual Property Rights created or developed in the performance of this Agreement, whether solely by a Party or jointly.

* “**Goods**” means the goods described in the Pricing Schedule, as may be amended from time to time.

* “**GTCs**” means Buyer’s General Terms and Conditions of Purchase as in force from time to time.

* “**Intellectual Property Rights**” or “**IPR**” means all patents, copyrights, design rights, trade marks, trade secrets and other intellectual or industrial property rights worldwide whether registered or unregistered.

* “**Order**” means a purchase order issued by Buyer for Goods.

* “**Services**” means the related services described in the Pricing Schedule or any Order.

* “**Term**” has the meaning given in clause 3.

1.2 Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation.

1.3 Where any conflict arises between this Agreement and the GTCs, the **GTCs shall prevail**.

## 2. Appointment

2.1 Buyer appoints Supplier on a non-exclusive basis to supply the Goods and Services during the Term.

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

Name: Title: Date:

---
## Footnotes

## 2. Appointment

2.1 Buyer appoints Supplier on a non-exclusive basis to supply the Goods and Services during the Term.

2.2 Buyer makes no commitment to any minimum volume or value of Orders. Forecasts provided by Buyer are non-binding indications only.

## 3. Term

3.1 This Agreement commences on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of **three (3) years** unless terminated earlier in accordance with clause 18 (the “**Initial Term**”).

3.2 The Initial Term may be extended for a further period of up to two (2) years by mutual written agreement, such extension to be on the same terms as this Agreement unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

## 4. Orders

4.1 Buyer may issue Orders by any reasonable means including electronically. Each Order shall be deemed to incorporate this Agreement and the GTCs.

4.2 Supplier shall acknowledge each Order within two (2) Business Days. Failure to acknowledge within that period shall constitute acceptance of the Order on the terms set out therein.

4.3 Buyer may cancel or amend any Order at any time prior to despatch without liability to Supplier other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of cancellation, capped at fifteen percent (15%) of the Order value.

## 5. Pricing and Rebates

5.1 Prices are as set out in the Pricing Schedule and are exclusive of VAT.

5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.

5.3 Rebates shall accrue and be paid as set out in the Pricing Schedule.

5.4 Buyer reserves the right to benchmark prices at any time and to require Supplier to match any verified third-party offer for substantially comparable Goods.

## 6. Invoicing and Payment

6.1 Supplier shall invoice Buyer on a monthly basis in arrears.

6.2 Payment terms are **ninety (90) days** from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.

6.3 Where any sum is disputed, payment of the disputed portion may be withheld pending resolution; the undisputed balance shall be paid in accordance with clause 6.2.

6.4 Interest on overdue undisputed sums shall accrue at the statutory rate under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998. The parties agree that this rate represents a substantial remedy under section 8 of that Act.

## 7. Delivery and Risk

7.1 Delivery shall be Incoterms 2020 DDP to the receiving site nominated in the Order.

7.2 Risk in the Goods passes to Buyer on signed acceptance at the receiving site. Title passes on payment in full in accordance with clause 6.

7.3 Supplier shall meet the service levels in the SLA. Failure to meet OTIF targets in any three (3) consecutive months shall constitute material breach for the purposes of clause 18.2.

## 8. Quality and Acceptance

8.1 Goods shall conform to the specification, applicable industry standards, and any sample provided.

8.2 Buyer may inspect and test Goods at any reasonable time. Non-conforming Goods may be rejected within thirty (30) days of delivery, at Supplier’s cost and risk.

8.3 Supplier warrants Goods against defects in materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months from acceptance.

## 9. Intellectual Property

9.1 Each Party retains ownership of its Background IP.

9.2 **All Foreground IP** — including any modifications, improvements, derivatives, or jointly developed materials arising in connection with this Agreement — **shall vest in Buyer absolutely on creation**. Supplier hereby assigns to Buyer with full title guarantee all such Foreground IP, including future rights.

9.3 Supplier shall, at Buyer’s cost, do all such acts and execute all such documents as Buyer may reasonably require to perfect Buyer’s title.

9.4 Supplier grants Buyer a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, worldwide, sublicensable licence to use Supplier’s Background IP to the extent embedded in or required to exploit any Goods or Foreground IP.

## 10. Indemnity

10.1 **Supplier shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Buyer, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees and agents** from and against any and all claims, losses, damages, liabilities, costs and expenses (including reasonable legal fees on a full indemnity basis) arising out of or in connection with:

* any breach by Supplier of this Agreement;

* any negligent act or omission of Supplier or its personnel;

* any defect in the Goods or Services;

* any infringement or alleged infringement of any third-party Intellectual Property Right; and

* any breach by Supplier of any applicable law or regulation.

10.2 The indemnities in this clause 10 are uncapped and survive termination.

## 11. Limitation of Liability

11.1 Subject always to clause 10 (which is uncapped), Supplier’s aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement and any Order, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), misrepresentation or otherwise, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11.2 **Buyer’s aggregate liability** under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, **shall in no event exceed £100,000.**

11.3 Neither Party shall be liable for indirect, consequential or pure economic loss; provided that this exclusion shall not apply to (i) Supplier’s indemnity obligations under clause 10, (ii) breach of confidentiality, or (iii) breach of clauses 13, 15 or 16.

## 12. Insurance

12.1 Supplier shall maintain at its own cost throughout the Term and for six (6) years thereafter:

* public and product liability insurance of not less than £10 million per claim and in the aggregate;

* professional indemnity insurance of not less than £5 million per claim and in the aggregate; and

* employer’s liability insurance to the statutory minimum.

12.2 Supplier shall provide certificates of insurance on request and shall give Buyer thirty (30) days’ notice of any material change to its cover.

## 13. Compliance

13.1 Supplier shall comply with the Compliance and ESG Annex (Document 06), which forms part of this Agreement.

13.2 Supplier represents and warrants that:

* it complies with the Modern Slavery Act 2015 and maintains a current modern slavery statement;

* it complies with the Bribery Act 2010 and maintains adequate procedures within the meaning of section 7 of that Act;

* it complies with all applicable sanctions, export control and anti-money-laundering laws;

* neither it, its Affiliates, nor any of its personnel involved in performing this Agreement is a Sanctioned Person or located in a Sanctioned Jurisdiction; and

* it maintains and shall maintain an environmental management system certified to ISO 14001 or equivalent.

13.3 Supplier shall provide an annual ESG attestation in the form prescribed in the Compliance Annex, signed at director level.

## 14. Confidentiality

14.1 Each Party shall treat the other’s Confidential Information as confidential and use it only for the purposes of this Agreement.

14.2 The obligations of confidence in clause 14.1 shall continue indefinitely.

14.3 The confidentiality obligations shall not apply to information that is (i) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving Party; (ii) lawfully received from a third party without restriction; or (iii) required to be disclosed by law or regulatory authority.

## 15. Data Protection

15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer’s Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.

## 16. Audit Rights

16.1 Buyer may, on giving five (5) Business Days’ written notice, audit Supplier’s records, premises, systems and personnel insofar as they relate to Supplier’s performance of this Agreement, compliance with clause 13, or pricing under clause 5.

16.2 Audits may be conducted by Buyer or by a third-party auditor of Buyer’s choosing. Supplier shall provide reasonable cooperation and access at its own cost.

16.3 Audit rights survive termination for a period of six (6) years.

## 17. Force Majeure

17.1 Neither Party shall be liable for failure or delay in performance caused by an event beyond its reasonable control, provided that the affected Party gives prompt written notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate.

17.2 If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, **Buyer** (but not Supplier) may terminate this Agreement on written notice without liability.

## 18. Termination

18.1 **Termination for Convenience.** Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time **without cause** on **thirty (30) days’ written notice** to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.

18.2 **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement on written notice if the other Party (i) commits a material breach that is not remediable, or is remediable but is not remedied within thirty (30) days of written notice; (ii) becomes insolvent; or (iii) undergoes a change of control to a competitor of Buyer.

18.3 On termination Supplier shall, at no charge, transfer to Buyer or a nominated replacement supplier all data, materials, and Foreground IP relating to the Goods and Services.

## 19. Governing Law and Jurisdiction

19.1 This Agreement and any dispute arising out of or in connection with it shall be governed by **English law**.

19.2 The Parties submit to the **exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Manchester**, England.

## 20. General

20.1 **Order of precedence.** In the event of any conflict between (a) this Agreement, (b) the GTCs, (c) the SLA, (d) the Compliance Annex, or (e) any Order, the order of precedence shall be (b), (a), (d), (c), (e). For the avoidance of doubt, **the GTCs prevail over this Agreement**.

20.2 **Assignment.** Buyer may assign this Agreement or any rights under it to any Affiliate or to any successor in business. Supplier may not assign or subcontract without Buyer’s prior written consent.

20.3 **Variation.** No variation is effective unless in writing and signed by an authorised representative of each Party.

20.4 **Entire agreement.** This Agreement, together with the documents referred to in clause 20.1, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties.

20.5 **Notices.** Notices shall be given in writing to the addresses set out at the head of this Agreement.

20.6 **Third-party rights.** A person who is not a Party has no right under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement, save that Affiliates of Buyer may enforce clauses 9, 10, 13, 14, 15 and 16.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF** the Parties have executed this Agreement on the date set out at the head of this Agreement.

Signed for and on behalf of **Pemberton Engineering Ltd.**:

Name: Title: Date:

Signed for and on behalf of **[BIDDER NAME]**:

Name: Title: Date:

---

contents.text:
<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

changes.comment: REDLINE: Added survival post-termination and clarified scope limitation.
REDLINE: Made indemnities mutual and added cap (subject to standard carve-outs for death, fraud, wilful misconduct).

changes.target_text: 11.2 Buyers aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, shall in no event exceed £100,000.
changes.new_text: 11.2 Each Party's aggregate liability under or in connection with this Agreement, howsoever arising, shall not exceed an amount equal to the fees paid or payable by Buyer to Supplier in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.
changes.comment: REDLINE: Symmetrised liability caps — both sides capped at 12 months' fees.

changes.target_text: 6.2 Payment terms are ninety (90) days from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.
changes.new_text: 6.2 Payment terms are sixty (60) days from receipt of a valid undisputed invoice.
changes.comment: REDLINE: Reduced payment terms from 90 to 60 days — market standard.

changes.target_text: 5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Buyer shall have absolute discretion to accept or reject any proposed adjustment.
changes.new_text: 5.2 Prices are firm for the first twelve (12) months of the Term. Thereafter Supplier may propose annual price adjustments capped at the lower of UK CPI and 2.5%. Any proposed adjustment shall require the mutual written agreement of both Parties.
changes.comment: REDLINE: Removed buyer's unilateral discretion — price changes require mutual agreement.

changes.target_text: 15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex available on request from Buyer's Data Protection Officer, which shall be deemed to be incorporated into this Agreement on its current terms from time to time.
changes.new_text: 15.1 The Parties shall comply with the data processing terms set out in the Data Processing Annex in the form attached as Schedule [X] to this Agreement. No variations to the Data Processing Annex shall be effective except by written agreement signed by both Parties.
changes.comment: REDLINE: Fixed DPA version (no incorporation 'from time to time') and added variation control.

changes.target_text: 18.1 Termination for Convenience. Buyer may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time without cause on thirty (30) days written notice to Supplier. No early-termination charges shall be payable other than reimbursement of demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred up to the date of termination, capped at five percent (5%) of the value of work-in-progress on the termination date.
changes.new_text: 18.1 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement or any Order at any time without cause on ninety (90) days written notice to the other Party. Upon termination by either Party under this clause 18.1, Buyer shall pay Supplier: (a) the value of all Goods delivered but not yet paid for; (b) demonstrable direct costs reasonably incurred on committed inventory for which Supplier cannot reasonably find an alternative outlet; and (c) a reasonable demobilisation fee to cover documented wind-down costs, in each case supported by reasonable documentary evidence.
changes.comment: REDLINE: Made termination right reciprocal and enhanced compensation to cover committed inventory and demobilisation costs.
